meta.title: Smart Energy — HMI Evaluation (XR / Quest3 condition)
meta.description: Operator performs the same structured N-1 security assessment workflow using the XR HMI on Meta Quest3. Paired with pilot_se_hmi_eval_2d_v0 for the 2D vs XR comparison study. Same workflow, same events, different interface and input modality.

task_parameters.note_rt_max: Same domain task as 2D condition — rt_max_s must match for metrics to be comparable across conditions.
task_parameters.note_baseline: Same baseline as 2D condition. Keeping it equal isolates the interface effect in EL.

profile.note: Same AI backend as 2D condition — model is unchanged between conditions.
Active only when operator explicitly invokes at step 10. In XR this may be a voice or gesture trigger — log as agent_invoked regardless of modality.

objects.name: HMI_XR
objects.note: Affordances map to the same event names as the 2D condition. input_modality:xr_raycast in payload differentiates from 2D clicks.

workflow.description: Same 10-step protocol as pilot_se_hmi_eval_2d_v0. Event names are identical — only payload.environment and payload.input_modality differ. This is intentional: the comparison is between interfaces, not between different workflows.

steps.label: Open environment
steps.note: App launch = session boundary. start_time already present in IML JSON.

steps.label: Select simulation timestamp
steps.note: Context for the run. Goes into payload.scenario_datetime of simulation_triggered.

steps.label: Inspect base case
steps.note: In XR the operator may spatially rotate around the grid visualisation. duration_s = from render to next gesture/action.

steps.label: Apply contingency (select line)
steps.note: interaction_id starts here. In XR: operator points ray-cast at a line on the 3D grid map.

steps.label: Run N-1 contingency
steps.note: IML fires simulation_triggered. UBI fires n1_analysis_completed when backend returns. Same interaction_id as step 5.

steps.label: Inspect line results
steps.note: In XR: operator may look at colour-coded lines in 3D space. Dwell time is the key metric here for the 2D vs XR comparison.

steps.label: Inspect voltage results
steps.note: In XR: may be a spatial layer toggle. Each toggle that results in dwell = one result_inspected event.

steps.label: Identify violations (select element)
steps.note: input_modality:xr_raycast is the payload field that distinguishes this from the 2D condition.

steps.label: Consult Operator Agent
steps.note: In XR the operator may invoke the agent via voice command or spatial gesture. Log as agent_invoked regardless of input modality. Operator decision event closes the cycle.

xr_specific.note: Fields below are XR-only additions to the payload. They are optional for the HAIC metrics but useful for the XR usability analysis.

optional_payload_fields.head_rotation_deg: Degrees of head rotation during inspection step — proxy for spatial engagement
optional_payload_fields.controller_activations: Number of ray-cast trigger events during a dwell period
optional_payload_fields.layer_switches: Number of display layer toggles (line vs voltage vs base case)

expected_differences.D: XR may show higher dwell time on spatial inspection steps (7,8) — operator explores 3D space
expected_differences.F: 2D may show higher event frequency — familiar interface, faster navigation
expected_differences.HCL: XR cognitive load unclear a priori — novelty effect vs spatial intuitiveness
expected_differences.TR: Trust comparison requires sufficient cycles with agent interaction at step 10
expected_differences.EL: If XR reduces decision time vs 2D, EL will be lower for XR — the key research question

metrics_of_interest: HCL (cognitive load proxy — normalised by rt_max_s=120)
